ops.title: User's env sets UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER to a short rolling window (~1h), so uvx/uv exclude just-published packages — causes \"no version X==N\" + stale resolves right after a release; prefix env -u UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER to pick up fresh publishes
ops.body:
The user's environment sets `UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER` to a short rolling window (seen as `1h`), which makes uv/uvx ignore any package version published after `now − window`. Right after you publish a release this produces confusing failures: `uvx --from 'pkg==N.N.N'` reports "no version of pkg==N.N.N" (the just-published version is excluded), and a bare `uvx pkg` resolves to a stale/older dependency combination instead of the new release. `--refresh` does NOT help — it refreshes the index cache but still honors the exclude-newer cutoff.

**Why:** Discovered 2026-06-18 cutting capt-hook v3.9.1: a fresh `uvx capt-hook` resolved to the wrong deps (pulled a broken spawnllm and missed the new release), and `uvx --from capt-hook==3.9.1` was "unsatisfiable" until `UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER` was unset. captain-hook's own hook wiring already works around this with `env -u UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER uv run …`.

**How to apply:** To install/test/run a just-published package — or to verify a release you just cut — prefix the command with `env -u UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER`. BUT for `uv tool install`, `env -u` alone is often NOT enough: it can still resolve from a stale cached index and silently install the *previous* version (seen 2026-06-23 cutting cookiesync-cli v0.2.0 — `env -u UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER uv tool install --force cookiesync-cli` installed 0.1.5). The reliable form pins the exact version and refreshes the cache: `env -u UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER uv tool install --force --refresh 'pkg==N.N.N'` — pinning makes it fail loudly instead of falling back, and `--refresh` busts the stale index. (The earlier "--refresh doesn't help" note was about the exclude-newer *cutoff*; the cutoff and the *index cache* are two separate blockers — unset the env var AND refresh.) Never conclude a release is broken or a version is "missing from PyPI" based on a uv/uvx resolution failure until you've retried with `UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER` unset + `--refresh` + an exact pin. Separate third blocker (seen 2026-07-12 cutting capt-hook v9.5.0, with the env var NOT set): a bare `uvx pkg` — the form plugin hooks run — keeps serving its **cached tool environment** (the previous release) for a while after publish even though a pinned `uvx pkg@N.N.N` resolves the new one; `uv cache clean pkg` is not a reliable unstick because it contends on the global `~/.cache/uv/.lock` with every concurrent session's hook uvx runs (300s timeout observed). It self-heals within the metadata TTL — verify releases with an exact pin and let bare-uvx consumers catch up on their own. Related: [[plugin-cache-keys-on-version]], [[always-push-commits]], [[cc-pool-config-copies]].
